pr.title: Split text.s into 2 parts to pull out some PPC EABI Runtime files
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Split the monolithic assembly text section into multiple assembly files so known CodeWarrior/PPC EABI runtime routines could be isolated and named. The PR introduced dedicated asm files for runtime support such as Gecko_setjmp, NMWException, __init_cpp_exceptions, __va_arg, and runtime.s, moved most remaining text into asm/text_2.s, left a small asm/text_1.s boundary region, updated ldscript.lcf, and renamed callsites from generic func_80322xxx labels to runtime helper names like __cvt_fp2unsigned, __cvt_dbl_usll, __cvt_sll_dbl, __div2i, __shl2i, and __shr2u.
what_changed: Split the prior large text assembly into asm/text_1.s and asm/text_2.s to create boundaries around identified PPC EABI runtime code.
Added asm/runtime.s containing extracted runtime helper code; the context only gives file-level evidence and callsite names, not the full contents.
Added asm/Gecko_setjmp.s with __setjmp and longjmp implementations saving/restoring LR, CR, r1/r2, GPRs r13-r31, FPRs f14-f31, and FPSCR state.
Added asm/NMWException.s with __destroy_global_chain, __unregister_fragment, and __register_fragment symbols for global destructor chain / exception fragment registration logic.
Added asm/__init_cpp_exceptions.s with GetR2, __init_cpp_exceptions, and __fini_cpp_exceptions around fragment registration and unregistering state stored in SDA labels.
Added asm/__va_arg.s with the __va_arg runtime implementation.

Updated callsites in existing assembly files to replace generic labels with identified runtime helper names, including func_803228C0 to __cvt_fp2unsigned, func_80322E54 to __cvt_dbl_usll, func_80322DA0 to __cvt_sll_dbl, func_80322A08 to __div2i, func_80322D30 to __shl2i, and func_80322D54 to __shr2u.
Updated ldscript.lcf to account for the newly separated assembly files and text boundaries.
Touched asm/aobj.s, asm/cobj.s, asm/code_80008D30.s, asm/code_8037E708.s, and asm/perf.s primarily to update branch target symbol names after runtime extraction.
smart_moves: Created file boundaries around known vendor/runtime code instead of leaving all code in a single monolithic text.s, making future decompilation and library identification easier.
Used recognized CodeWarrior/PPC EABI routine names for extracted symbols, improving semantic searchability and reducing reliance on address-based func_ labels.
Preserved address-annotated assembly while only changing labels and file organization, which is a low-risk way to improve structure without changing machine code intent.
Renamed callsites across unrelated game/engine assembly to point at the newly identified runtime helpers, making cross-file dependencies explicit.

Separated standard support routines such as setjmp/longjmp, varargs handling, C++ exception initialization/finalization, and integer/float conversion helpers into dedicated files that can later be matched against known runtime libraries.
decomp_lessons: When a contiguous block matches known compiler runtime code, isolate it into vendor/runtime-specific assembly files before attempting higher-level decompilation.
Runtime library recognition can unlock many generic func_ labels at once because game code often calls the same conversion, division, shift, varargs, and exception helpers repeatedly.
Linker script updates are part of assembly splitting work; file boundaries must preserve original section order and addresses.
Callsite relabeling is valuable even before full C decompilation because it documents ABI/runtime semantics and improves later matching efforts.

For CodeWarrior/PPC EABI support code, look for clusters around __va_arg, __setjmp/longjmp, __register_fragment, __unregister_fragment, __init_cpp_exceptions, __fini_cpp_exceptions, GetR2, __div2i, __shl2i, __shr2u, __cvt_fp2unsigned, __cvt_dbl_usll, and __cvt_sll_dbl.
naming_conventions: Use standard runtime names with leading double underscores for compiler support helpers: __va_arg, __setjmp, __init_cpp_exceptions, __fini_cpp_exceptions, __register_fragment, __unregister_fragment, __destroy_global_chain.
Use known conversion helper names instead of address-derived names: __cvt_fp2unsigned, __cvt_dbl_usll, __cvt_sll_dbl.
Use known 64-bit arithmetic helper names instead of generic func labels: __div2i, __shl2i, __shr2u.
GetR2 was capitalized exactly as shown in the extracted __init_cpp_exceptions assembly.
Assembly file names mirror recognized runtime modules/symbols, including Gecko_setjmp.s, NMWException.s, __init_cpp_exceptions.s, and __va_arg.s.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Identify PPC EABI runtime functions by instruction patterns and ABI behavior: setjmp saves LR/CR/r1/r2, GPR nonvolatiles, FPR nonvolatiles, and FPSCR; longjmp restores them and returns 1 when the provided value is zero.
Use C++ exception fragment registration patterns to identify __register_fragment, __unregister_fragment, __init_cpp_exceptions, and __fini_cpp_exceptions, including SDA-stored state and GetR2 usage.
Recognize varargs machinery by register-save-area and overflow-area manipulation in __va_arg.
After extracting a runtime region, update all branch labels in remaining assembly so calls refer to semantic runtime symbols while preserving original branch opcodes/addresses.
Use a split text_1/text_2 approach when only a middle runtime block is ready to extract and the rest of the text section still needs to remain assembly.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that decompile or replace asm/runtime.s with C sources.
Find later PRs touching __va_arg or CodeWarrior varargs ABI structs.
Find later PRs that further split text_1.s or text_2.s into named library files.
Find later PRs involving PPC EABI helpers __div2i, __shl2i, __shr2u, __cvt_fp2unsigned, __cvt_dbl_usll, or __cvt_sll_dbl.
Find linker script changes that depended on this runtime extraction.
